message.reasoning_content:
Let me analyze the input. I have one round/part called "Button" with the instruction "Weave in the ends." and title "Weave in ends".

This is a non-stitch bookkeeping instruction - it's an assembly/finishing action. Let me convert it to Crochet IR.

The instruction "Weave in the ends." is a finishing action, so it should be a bookkeeping operation with actionTag "assembly" or something like "weaveInEnds". Let me check the recommended values for bookkeeping: turn, skip, joinYarn, fastenOff, changeColor, setWorkingLoop, placeMarker, removeMarker, moveMarker, assembly, custom.

"Weave in the ends" sounds like an assembly/finishing action. I'll use "assembly" as the actionTag and put a descriptive instruction.

No expectedProducedStitches since this isn't a stitch-producing round.
